name: Service Layer + Repository
description: Separate transport business logic and persistence concerns
when_to_use: Most backend services and coding-agent implementations
trade_offs: Can become ceremony for very small scripts
implementation_notes: Controller validates and maps service owns business rules repository owns queries
keywords: service layer repository controller clean architecture

name: ACID transaction boundary
description: Group related writes so they succeed or fail atomically
when_to_use: Multi-table writes payments inventory bookings account changes
trade_offs: Long transactions can lock rows and reduce throughput
implementation_notes: Start transaction in service layer keep it short and use retries for serialization conflicts
keywords: transaction acid multi table write consistency

name: N+1 prevention
description: Batch or join related data instead of querying inside loops
when_to_use: List endpoints GraphQL resolvers dashboards and exports
trade_offs: Overfetching can happen if joins are too broad
implementation_notes: Use eager loading batched IN queries DataLoader or precomputed read models
keywords: n+1 query dataloader eager loading loop

name: Query-driven indexing
description: Create indexes from actual filter sort and join patterns
when_to_use: Endpoints with latency targets and growing tables
trade_offs: Indexes speed reads but slow writes and consume storage
implementation_notes: Use composite indexes matching where/order clauses and inspect query plans
keywords: index composite query plan postgres mysql

name: Tenant isolation with scoped access
description: Multi-tenant SaaS with shared application infrastructure
when_to_use: Shared schema is cheaper but raises isolation risk
trade_offs: Apply tenant_id at repository boundary and enforce RLS where supported
implementation_notes: multi tenant tenant_id row level security saas

name: Optimistic concurrency control
description: Detect conflicting writes through version columns or timestamps
when_to_use: Collaborative editing inventory booking and account updates
trade_offs: Conflicts need user or retry handling
implementation_notes: Include version in update predicate and return conflict on mismatch

name: CQRS read model
description: Separate write model from optimized read projections
when_to_use: Complex dashboards search-heavy systems and event-driven domains
trade_offs: Adds projection lag and operational complexity
implementation_notes: Use only where read/write needs diverge materially

name: Partitioning and sharding
description: Split data by range hash or tenant for scale or isolation
when_to_use: Very large tables high write throughput tenant-level scaling
trade_offs: Operational complexity migrations and cross-shard queries
implementation_notes: Prefer partitioning before sharding and define shard key from access patterns
keywords: sharding partitioning scale tenant high throughput

name: Database constraints as invariants
description: Enforce uniqueness foreign keys and check constraints in the database
when_to_use: Critical data integrity such as orders payments ledgers and tenant ownership
trade_offs: Requires planned migrations and error mapping
implementation_notes: Use constraints as final guard after application validation
keywords: unique constraint foreign key check invariant integrity

name: Backward-compatible migrations
description: Deploy schema changes without downtime
when_to_use: Production services with rolling deploys and active traffic
trade_offs: Requires expand migrate contract sequencing
implementation_notes: Add nullable columns backfill dual-write then enforce and remove old fields

name: Pessimistic row locking
description: Lock selected rows for critical concurrent updates
when_to_use: Inventory booking wallet balance and single resource scheduling
trade_offs: Can reduce throughput and deadlock if overused
implementation_notes: Use select for update in short transactions with stable lock ordering
keywords: row lock select for update inventory booking

name: Read replica routing
description: Send safe read traffic to replicas to reduce primary load
when_to_use: Read-heavy applications dashboards and reports
trade_offs: Replica lag can return stale data
implementation_notes: Keep read-your-write flows on primary and expose lag metrics
keywords: read replica lag primary dashboard report

name: Soft delete with lifecycle policy
description: Mark records deleted instead of immediate physical deletion
when_to_use: User data recovery audit requirements and compliance workflows
trade_offs: Can complicate uniqueness and query filters
implementation_notes: Add deleted_at filters partial indexes and purge policy

name: Append-only ledger
description: Represent money or points as immutable debit credit entries
when_to_use: Payments wallets credits subscriptions and accounting
trade_offs: Queries require aggregation or snapshots
implementation_notes: Never update balances alone derive from entries and reconcile
keywords: ledger append only balance payment accounting

name: Search index projection
description: Project searchable data into Elasticsearch OpenSearch or Meilisearch
when_to_use: Full-text search faceting and typo tolerance
trade_offs: Index can lag source of truth
implementation_notes: Use database as source of truth and rebuildable projections

name: Restore-tested backups
description: Backups are validated by regular restore drills
when_to_use: Any production data store
trade_offs: Restore tests consume time and storage
implementation_notes: Automate restore verification and document RPO RTO
keywords: backup restore rpo rto database disaster recovery

name: Aggregate boundary
description: Group data changes around domain aggregates
when_to_use: Domain-driven systems orders accounts inventory and booking
trade_offs: Wrong boundaries cause large transactions or weak invariants
implementation_notes: Define aggregate root and enforce invariants in service layer

name: Reference data table
description: Store stable controlled values as data not enums in code
when_to_use: Configurable product categories countries plans and statuses
trade_offs: Overuse creates joins for simple constants
implementation_notes: Use when values change without deploy or need metadata

name: Temporal table
description: Track historical state over time
when_to_use: Audit history pricing policies and compliance records
trade_offs: Queries become more complex
implementation_notes: Use valid_from valid_to or database temporal features

name: Immutable event table
description: Store facts as append-only events
when_to_use: Audit trails event sourcing and integrations
trade_offs: Requires projections for current state
implementation_notes: Append only with event type aggregate id version and metadata
keywords: event table append only event sourcing

name: Keyset pagination index
description: Support cursor pagination with stable index
when_to_use: List endpoints over large tables
trade_offs: Requires deterministic ordering
implementation_notes: Index order columns plus primary key tie breaker

name: Covering index
description: Serve query from index without table lookup
when_to_use: High-traffic read endpoints and narrow projections
trade_offs: More storage and slower writes
implementation_notes: Include selected columns only when query is hot

name: Partial index
description: Index a filtered subset of rows
when_to_use: Soft deletes active records and status filtered queries
trade_offs: Only helps queries matching predicate
implementation_notes: Create partial indexes for common predicates like deleted_at is null

name: Materialized view
description: Precompute expensive read queries
when_to_use: Dashboards reporting and expensive aggregations
trade_offs: Refresh lag and storage overhead
implementation_notes: Refresh on schedule or event and expose freshness

name: Unique idempotency table
description: Store idempotency keys with request hash and result
when_to_use: Payment checkout import and webhook writes
trade_offs: Requires retention cleanup
implementation_notes: Use unique key per actor operation and replay stored response

name: Advisory lock
description: Coordinate application-level critical section in database
when_to_use: Rare scheduled jobs and per-resource mutual exclusion
trade_offs: Can deadlock or leak if misused
implementation_notes: Use transaction-scoped advisory locks and timeout

name: Serializable transaction retry
description: Guarantee correctness under high concurrency
when_to_use: Financial ledgers inventory and booking conflicts
trade_offs: Retries can increase latency
implementation_notes: Use serializable isolation with bounded retry loop

name: Connection pool sizing
description: Prevent database overload and request queue collapse
when_to_use: Every production service using relational databases
trade_offs: Too many connections hurt database performance
implementation_notes: Size pools per instance and total cluster capacity

name: Connection proxy
description: Share database connections across serverless or many app instances
when_to_use: Serverless high instance counts and bursty workloads
trade_offs: Adds network hop and proxy dependency
implementation_notes: Use PgBouncer or managed proxy with transaction pooling

name: Hot partition avoidance
description: Prevent one key from receiving most reads or writes
when_to_use: Multi-tenant SaaS queues counters and time-series tables
trade_offs: Requires key design and sometimes bucketing
implementation_notes: Shard hot tenants or bucket high-write keys

name: OLTP OLAP separation
description: Keep transactional and analytical workloads separate
when_to_use: Apps with reports BI and large scans
trade_offs: Data pipelines introduce lag
implementation_notes: Replicate to warehouse or read replica for analytics

name: CDC replication
description: Stream database changes to downstream systems
when_to_use: Search indexing analytics cache invalidation and integrations
trade_offs: Schema changes and ordering need care
implementation_notes: Use CDC connector and schema evolution policy

name: PII classification columns
description: Mark and control sensitive fields in schema
when_to_use: Regulated domains and privacy programs
trade_offs: Requires process and tooling
implementation_notes: Document sensitivity retention encryption and logging policy per field

name: Data retention purge job
description: Delete or anonymize data according to retention policy
when_to_use: Compliance privacy and storage control
trade_offs: Deletion can break audits if not modeled
implementation_notes: Define retention per table and run auditable purge jobs

name: Online backfill
description: Backfill large tables without locking production
when_to_use: Schema migrations derived columns and denormalization
trade_offs: Backfill can compete with live workload
implementation_notes: Process in chunks with checkpoints throttling and resume

name: Migration lock timeout
description: Fail migrations instead of blocking production traffic
when_to_use: Production relational schema changes
trade_offs: Requires retry and migration review
implementation_notes: Set lock timeout statement timeout and avoid long table rewrites

name: Foreign key cascade discipline
description: Use cascades deliberately for lifecycle-owned data
when_to_use: Parent-child records with clear ownership
trade_offs: Accidental cascade can delete too much data
implementation_notes: Prefer restrict for critical records and document cascade ownership

name: Optimistic unique create
description: Use unique constraints to handle concurrent create races
when_to_use: Slug username invite and membership creation
trade_offs: Requires mapping constraint errors to domain errors
implementation_notes: Insert and catch unique violation instead of precheck only

name: Migration smoke test
description: Apply migrations against realistic schema in CI
when_to_use: Teams shipping frequent database changes
trade_offs: Needs seeded schema snapshots
implementation_notes: Run migrate up and basic query smoke before deploy

name: Query plan regression check
description: Detect query plan or index regressions before production
when_to_use: Critical high-volume endpoints
trade_offs: Plans vary across data distributions
implementation_notes: Store representative explain plans and watch row estimates
